query: Generate a benchmark-stable vtk.js scene that can seed later task-specific benchmark cases vtk.js Stable Benchmark Scaffold Task Favor explicit acceptance checks and repeatable workflow inputs over visual complexity.

search.query: Generate a benchmark-stable vtk.js scene that can seed later task-specific benchmark cases vtk.js Stable Benchmark Scaffold Task Favor explicit acceptance checks and repeatable workflow inputs over visual complexity.

papers.title: Benchmark-Oriented vtk.js Validation Cases
papers.abstract: Repeatable benchmark cases should cover slice, volume rendering, streamline, and mag-iso scenes with stable artifact expectations.

papers.title: Streamline and Vector Field Scene Construction in vtk.js
papers.abstract: Streamline tasks typically require seeded sources, vector field inputs, mapper wiring, and explicit render ordering to avoid empty scenes.

papers.title: Recovering from Missing Canvas during vtk.js Verification
papers.abstract: A missing canvas usually indicates that render bootstrap failed, so retry with a minimal sphere scene before reintroducing custom logic.

papers.title: Browser Evidence for vtk.js Validation with Playwright
papers.abstract: Reliable vtk.js validation should capture screenshots, browser console logs, page errors, and a render verification verdict from Playwright.

papers.title: Repairing Missing vtk Runtime in Browser Validation
papers.abstract: When the browser reports vtk is not defined, inject the vtk.js browser bundle before the module script and keep the runtime bootstrap minimal.

papers.title: Slice Rendering and Image Data Patterns in vtk.js
papers.abstract: Slice workflows benefit from retrieving image data setup, slice mapper, actor configuration, and viewport constraints for validation.

snippets: Repeatable benchmark cases should cover slice, volume rendering, streamline, and mag-iso scenes with stable artifact expectations.
Streamline tasks typically require seeded sources, vector field inputs, mapper wiring, and explicit render ordering to avoid empty scenes.
A missing canvas usually indicates that render bootstrap failed, so retry with a minimal sphere scene before reintroducing custom logic.
Reliable vtk.js validation should capture screenshots, browser console logs, page errors, and a render verification verdict from Playwright.
When the browser reports vtk is not defined, inject the vtk.js browser bundle before the module script and keep the runtime bootstrap minimal.
A minimal browser scene should create a render window, source, mapper, actor, and then call renderWindow.render() after resetCamera.

recommendedPatterns: Use slice, volume, streamline, and mag-iso as the benchmark backbone for vtk.js eval.
Seed streamline scenes explicitly and confirm vector-field inputs before validation.
Use a minimal sphere or cone scene to re-establish canvas rendering before restoring custom logic.
Expect screenshot, console log, page error log, and a structured render verdict.
Treat browser evidence as the acceptance contract for vtk.js scene validation.
Inject the vtk.js browser bundle before any module script that references global vtk.
failureFixPairs: Capture artifact expectations per benchmark case so later eval output stays comparable.
If streamline output is empty, inspect seed generation and pipeline ordering before general browser repair.
If the canvas is missing, reduce the page to a known-good bootstrap scene and compare new artifacts.
If artifacts are missing, rerun the canonical Docker workflow instead of improvising a different browser command.
When the page says vtk is not defined, restore the runtime script include before changing scene logic.
If slice rendering regresses, compare mapper wiring and viewport setup against a minimal slice baseline.
